title: A rare case of common variable immunodeficiency and hepatopulmonary syndrome.
abstract: Hepatopulmonary syndrome (HPS) is the triad of liver disease, hypoxia and intrapulmonary shunt. In this case report, we describe an immunocompromised female with a background of common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) who presented with haemoptysis and dyspnoea. Investigations demonstrated significant hypoxaemia and intrapulmonary right-to-left shunting. Further evidence revealed advanced liver cirrhosis, and a diagnosis of HPS was made. This extremely rare association of CVID and HPS is one of the few cases reported in the literature.
